§6Advanced Circuits§f

§aHigher-tier circuit boards for reactors, warheads, and fusion.§f

All made in the Soldering Station with increasing power and fluid requirements.

§6Tiers§f:
§b• Integrated Circuit Board§f: Adds microchips. Requires arc furnace for silicon wafers. Used in geiger counters, missiles, mid-game machines.

§b• Military Grade Circuit Board§f: Requires sulfuric acid (1,000 mB) + rubber. Heavy capacitor/microchip use. Used in particle accelerators, nuclear warheads, advanced reactors.

§b• Versatile Circuit Board§f: Requires bismuth, hard plastic, solvent (1,000 mB). Extremely expensive (~55 insulators each). Used in fusion reactors, hydrotreaters.

§6Control Units§f:
§b• Control Unit§f: For mid-late nuclear weapons (Gadget). Needs control unit casing + capacitors + microchips + perfluoromethyl.
§b• Advanced Control Unit§f: For late nuclear weapons (Ivy Mike). Adds atomic clocks + speed upgrades + more perfluoromethyl.

§6Specialized§f:
§b• Capacitor Board: Tantalium + H₂O₂. Used in tectonic warheads.
§b• Quantum Processing Unit: Atomic clocks + quantum processors + He-4. For ICF reactor.

§eTip§f: Military Grade sees the widest use. Automate rubber, sulfuric acid, and tantalium before scaling up.
